nom.name: Nominative (1. pád)
nom.function: The subject case — who or what performs the action. Also used after 'to je' (this is).
nom.question: Kdo? Co? (Who? What?)

gen.name: Genitive (2. pád)
gen.function: Expresses possession, origin, absence, or quantity. Used after many prepositions (do, z, od, u, bez, etc.).
gen.question: Koho? Čeho? (Whom? Of what?)

dat.name: Dative (3. pád)
dat.function: The indirect object case — the person receiving something or benefiting from an action. Used with k/ke, proti, kvůli, díky, naproti.
dat.question: Komu? Čemu? (To whom? To what?)

acc.name: Accusative (4. pád)
acc.function: The direct object case — who or what is directly affected by the action. Used with na, pro, přes (direction).
acc.question: Koho? Co? (Whom? What?)

voc.name: Vocative (5. pád)
voc.function: Used for direct address — calling someone by name or title (e.g. 'Petře!', 'Maminko!', 'Pane!'). The only case not governed by prepositions.
voc.question: — (no question; used for calling)

loc.name: Locative (6. pád)
loc.function: Expresses location or the topic of discussion. Always used with a preposition (v, na, o, po, při).
loc.question: O kom? O čem? (About whom? About what?)

ins.name: Instrumental (7. pád)
ins.function: Expresses the means or instrument of action, accompaniment (with), or location behind/under/above. Also obligatory with 'stát se' (to become) to mark the resulting role: 'Stal se lékařem'. With 'být', the instrumental marks a role/function in formal or written register ('Byl učitelem'); modern colloquial Czech prefers the nominative when 'být' equates two nouns ('Jsem učitel'). Other verbs taking the role-instrumental: zůstat, jmenovat, zvolit.
ins.question: Kým? Čím? (By whom? With what?)
